from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a fluent single‑sentence description from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping of predicate to natural‑language phrase
    phrase_map = {
        "almaMater": "graduated from",
        "birthDate": "was born on",
        "birthName": "was born as",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "dateOfRetirement": "retired in",
        "occupation": "was a",
        "status": "has status",
        "timeInSpace": "spent",
        "mission": "participated in mission",
        "selectedByNasa": "was selected by NASA in",
        "deathDate": "died on",
        "nationality": "was a citizen of",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "served as chief of the astronaut office in",
        "title": "held the title",
        "ribbonAward": "received the ribbon award",
        "operator": "was operated by",
        "backupPilot": "had backup pilot",
        "commander": "had commander",
        "crewMembers": "had crew member",
        "representative": "was represented by",
        "alternativeName": "also known as",
        "awards": "received",
        "fossil": "has fossil",
        "senators": "has senators",
        "part": "is part of",
        "partsType": "has parts type",
        "higher": "is higher than",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "bird": "has bird",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "competeIn": "competes in",
        "president": "has president",
        "award": "received the award",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "gemstone": "has gemstone",
        "mascot": "has mascot",
        "cosparId": "has COSPAR ID",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset",
    }

    # Group objects by (subject, predicate)
    groups: dict[tuple[str, str], list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        key = (t.subject, t.predicate)
        groups.setdefault(key, []).append(t.object)

    # Helper to clean object strings (remove surrounding quotes)
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        o = o.strip()
        if (o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"')) or (o.startswith("'") and o.endswith("'")):
            return o[1:-1]
        return o

    # Build sentences per subject, merging predicates into a single clause
    sentences = []
    for subject in {subj for (subj, _) in groups}:
        # collect predicate phrases for this subject
        pred_phrases = []
        for (subj, pred), objs in groups.items():
            if subj != subject:
                continue
            phrase = phrase_map.get(pred, pred.replace("_", " "))
            cleaned_objs = [clean_obj(o) for o in objs]
            if len(cleaned_objs) == 1:
                obj_text = cleaned_objs[0]
            else:
                obj_text = ", ".join(cleaned_objs[:-1]) + " and " + cleaned_objs[-1]
            pred_phrases.append(f"{phrase} {obj_text}")

        # join predicate phrases with commas and an 'and' before the last item
        if len(pred_phrases) == 1:
            body = pred_phrases[0]
        else:
            body = ", ".join(pred_phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + pred_phrases[-1]

        sentences.append(f"{subject}, {body}")

    # Combine multiple subject sentences (if any) into one paragraph
    sentence = " ".join(sentences)
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
